ground_truth_requirements.text: Description In many countries there are certain regulations with regard to evidences that have to be considered. For example in some countries it is not allowed to directly issue an extract from the register of conviction to the economic operator but to directly deliver these evidence to the requesting authority. Requirement Special legal regulations for evidences MUST be considered (e.g. for extract from the register of conviction). Measurement Results of analysis of legal basis. Requirement Regulations for handling the non-existence of selected evidence requested in a MS MUST be defined and made operational. Measurement Results of analysis of legal basis. ground_truth_requirements.text: Description Data protection defines a legal basis for the handling of information relating to people or entities. Data protection is the main piece of legislation that governs the protection of personal or entity related data in a way in which individuals or entities can enforce the control of information about themselves. Although data protection does not directly refer to principles like data privacy, data confidentiality, data availability, non-repudiation of data, data integrity and data security these principles are strongly connected to data protection. Thus data protection plays an important role for a VCD. Requirement Relevant regulations of national data protection acts MUST be analysed and considered. Additional requirements for cross-border data exchange MUST be analysed and considered. Measurement Results of analysis of legal basis. Data protection is the main piece of legislation that governs the protection of personal or entity related data in a way in which individuals or entities can enforce the control of information about themselves. Although data protection does not directly refer to principles like data privacy, data confidentiality, data availability, non-repudiation of data, data integrity and data security these principles are strongly connected to data protection. Additional requirements for cross-border data exchange MUST be analysed and considered. Measurement Results of analysis of legal basis.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Description The VCD package is just one part of a bid that an economic operator may submit to a contracting authority. Therefore the rules and regulations in each Member State have to be analysed with regard to the prospects of separating these two parts from each other. This may also include for example to submit a VCD package via the PEPPOL infrastructure while submitting other parts or the bid itself via secondary channels or whether tendering platforms enable such separation. Requirement It MUST be clarified which prospects and limitations exists within a Member State to separate a VCD package from the tender itself. Measurement Results of analysis of legal basis. Therefore the rules and regulations in each Member State have to be analysed with regard to the prospects of separating these two parts from each other. This may also include for example to submit a VCD package via the PEPPOL infrastructure while submitting other parts or the bid itself via secondary channels or whether tendering platforms enable such separation. ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Description There are several actors that are involved in the processes related to WP2 and these processes have to be defined with regard to trust between the entities involved (e.g. European service provider, national service providers, issuing bodies, economic operators, contracting authorities). Trust models have to be defined for each process in order to clarify roles and responsibilities during transactions. This may include on a national level the definition of trust with regard to roles and responsibilities between national service providers and issuing bodies (public/private). On the European level it may include the “scope of mutual recognition” for example the trust between the national service provider who provides a VCD simple package and the European service provider who provides a particular mapping facility. Requirement A trust model and trust system MUST be defined from a legal point of view for every process which includes the roles and responsibilities of the entities involved. Measurement Ensuring trust through suitable legal regulations about roles, responsibilities and processes. Description There are several actors that are involved in the processes related to WP2 and these processes have to be defined with regard to trust between the entities involved (e.g. European service provider, national service providers, issuing bodies, economic operators, contracting authorities). Trust models have to be defined for each process in order to clarify roles and responsibilities during transactions. This may include on a national level the definition of trust with regard to roles and responsibilities between national service providers and issuing bodies (public/private). On the European level it may include the “scope of mutual recognition” for example the trust between the national service provider who provides a VCD simple package and the European service provider who provides a particular mapping facility. Requirement A trust model and trust system MUST be defined from a legal point of view for every process which includes the roles and responsibilities of the entities involved. Measurement Ensuring trust through suitable legal regulations about roles, responsibilities and processes.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Description “Some documents exchanged during the electronic procurement process have to be legally valid following EU Directives. Directive 2004/18 states that the public procurement procedures and the rules applicable to service contests require a level of security and confidentiality higher than that required by Directives. Accordingly, the devices for the electronic receipt of offers, requests to participate and plans and projects should comply with specific additional requirements. To this end, use of electronic signatures, in particular advanced electronic signatures, should, as far as possible, be encouraged. Moreover, the existence of voluntary accreditation schemes could constitute a favourable framework for enhancing the level of certification service provision for these devices.” (Bausa O. and Boujraf A. 2009) Requirement Electronic signature, with the same legal meaning of handwritten signature, SHOULD be encouraged in cross-border scenario. Requirement The regulations concerning the use of electronic documents, the exchange or download of electronic documents (e.g. mandatory usage of an e-signature) MUST be considered (especially regulations from national procurement act and directive 2004/18/EC). Measurement Results of analysis of legal basis for exchanging evidences and data. Requirement A process for document handling compliant with the relevant legal regulations MUST be defined (especially with respect to the data protection acts). Measurement Agreed process definition for document handling compliant with the relevant legal regulations. Description “Some documents exchanged during the electronic procurement process have to be legally valid following EU Directives. Directive 2004/18 states that the public procurement procedures and the rules applicable to service contests require a level of security and confidentiality higher than that required by Directives. Accordingly, the devices for the electronic receipt of offers, requests to participate and plans and projects should comply with specific additional requirements. To this end, use of electronic signatures, in particular advanced electronic signatures, should, as far as possible, be encouraged. Moreover, the existence of voluntary accreditation schemes could constitute a favourable framework for enhancing the level of certification service provision for these devices.” (Bausa O. and Boujraf A. 2009) Requirement Electronic signature, with the same legal meaning of handwritten signature, SHOULD be encouraged in cross-border scenario. Requirement The regulations concerning the use of electronic documents, the exchange or download of electronic documents (e.g. mandatory usage of an e-signature) MUST be considered (especially regulations from national procurement act and directive 2004/18/EC). Measurement Results of analysis of legal basis for exchanging evidences and data. Requirement A process for document handling compliant with the relevant legal regulations MUST be defined (especially with respect to the data protection acts). Measurement Agreed process definition for document handling compliant with the relevant legal regulations.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: This chapter shall include technical requirements that are essential for submitting a VCD via the PEPPOL infrastructures. In particular this refers to the proposed infrastructure of WP8 and the interrelation with our work package. The PEPPOL infrastructure of WP8 is dedicated in particular to the exchange of electronic business documents in a secure and reliable manner. Thereby it connects to existing e-business frameworks and value added networks and is being built on existing technologies and standards that have been tested and which have proven to be reliable and secure for enterprise use. WP2 takes a specific role within the project by concentrating on pre-awarding issues where requirements and procedures are often stricter due to principle like equal treatment, nondiscrimination, transparency, competition etc. Although a VCD does not include the offer itself it provides several documents that are of great importance for the award of contracts and therewith critical for the success of an economic operator for example in cases of disqualification because of late delivery of a VCD. An overall pre-condition for using the infrastructure and WP2 solutions in long term is that they are governed even after the project lifetime by maintaining and sustaining its functions. Therefore the governing models of WP8 and WP2 should be aligned. Authentication is an essential feature of WP2 as a Virtual Company Dossier contains several documents that contain sensitive data about an economic operator. Hence, it has to be ensured that only those persons that have sufficient rights are able to access and retrieve these data. Authentication of origin is also an important feature with regard to non-repudiation, for example when submitting a VCD to a contracting authority. Authentication is a verification of evidence of identity; for individuals and companies identifiers are used to process authentication. Such identifiers are national and in many countries specific and maintained within sectors, like tax, health, health insurance, employment, registration authorities, etc. PEPPOL will need to design an authentication system that serves its purposes. Hence, collaboration with WP 1 will be strengthened in the second phase of WP 2. Time-stamping shall ensure that the submission of a VCD is adequately documented for example to verify that it has been sent or received previously to the closure of a call for tender. In order to obtain trust, the VCD services should use certified time stamps. Moreover there are several actors involved in the WP2 scenarios which need to be connected to the PEPPOL infrastructure thus a sufficient allocation of identifiers, in connection with the identity an actor claims to have, has to be ensured. This shall ensure that unauthorized usage of an individuals’ identity is avoided. Overall we see a need for commonly monitored identity and access management in particular for all national VCD solutions and for the PEPPOL infrastructure thus both approaches should be aligned and should make use of the same mechanisms through for example single-sign-on functionality. Apart from functionalities like addressability and discovery, the infrastructure should provide access points to support the actors or services to connect to the infrastructure. The PEPPOL infrastructure will make it possible to exchange electronic business documents in a secure and reliable manner. Thus also the VCD should be able to be submitted through the PEPPOL infrastructure. We also assume that a VCD in stages 2 and 3 can be potentially submitted through other means but this is up to the choice of an economic operator and should not be promoted because bypassing the infrastructure is critical from the viewpoint of using validation services to ensure confidentiality of a VCD and its contained documents (WP1) and secure and reliable transport ensured through the PEPPOL infrastructure (WP8). During transportation the integrity of a VCD has to be ensured when for example validating it. Usage of the Infrastructure will require that national access points to the infrastructure (e.g. national service providers, contracting authorities) will be implemented but also direct access points that support SMEs in connecting to it. We also see a potential need to access web services via the infrastructure that enable client-side code generation in SOAP frameworks for example for the retrieval of attestations on demand, connecting to tendering platforms or viewing the VCD on some platform at the national service provider. ground_truth_requirements.text: A governing model has to be defined in order to deploy the WP2 systems and tools and to maintain their states and sustainability over time. In this respect the composition, structure and scope of the governing model has to be formally defined to ensure fulfilment of functions like liability, sustainability and maintainability (e.g. especially to maintain and sustain a pre-VCD mapping tool in long term and therewith to ensure (re-)liable functions) The dedicated governing model should have the necessary status and power to govern its assigned functions. Requirement It MUST be clarified how the governance model of WP2 fits into the overall governance model of WP8. Measurement Governing model of WP2. A governing model has to be defined in order to deploy the WP2 systems and tools and to maintain their states and sustainability over time. In this respect the composition, structure and scope of the governing model has to be formally defined to ensure fulfilment of functions like liability, sustainability and maintainability (e.g. especially to maintain and sustain a pre-VCD mapping tool in long term and therewith to ensure (re-)liable functions) The dedicated governing model should have the necessary status and power to govern its assigned functions. Requirement It MUST be clarified how the governance model of WP2 fits into the overall governance model of WP8. Measurement Governing model of WP2.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Description The identity and access management of systems for company evidential documents is regulated by local procedures and could become part of the WP2 proposed systems (cf. stage 2 description). Economic operators have to apply for a VCD account in order to become authenticated. An officer has to verify whether the data that is necessary to apply for an account belongs to the economic operator and has to assure its correctness. An economic operator can authenticate to the VCD system only when the economic operator has sufficient user rights provided by identity and access management. Thereby WP2 has to take into account which natural person has the power to act as authorized representative of the economic operator. Requirement WP2 stakeholders MUST be authenticated to the VCD system and for the PEPPOL infrastructure. Measurement Formal authentication mechanism. Requirement The identity and access management procedures for creating a VCD account SHOULD be harmonized with the registration procedure of the overall infrastructure provided by WP 8. Measurement Single-Sign-On functionality. Requirement A single-sign-on SHOULD be implemented in order to allow identity and access management across different systems. Measurement Single-Sign-On functionality. Measurement Single-Sign-On functionality.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Description Work package 2 will use the PEPPOL infrastructure for discovering addressing information which is typically stored in a registry. Therefore we need to find a method how addresses can be allocated and being provided to the WP 2 stakeholders so that they can use the PEPPOL infrastructure for exchanging VCDs and access related services. Requirement A method MUST be found how to allocate identifiers to the participants that use the infrastructure for transportation of VCDs and related services. Measurement Joined strategy and method with WP8 how to allocate identifiers among stakeholders and WP2 related services. Description Work package 2 will use the PEPPOL infrastructure for discovering addressing information which is typically stored in a registry. Therefore we need to find a method how addresses can be allocated and being provided to the WP 2 stakeholders so that they can use the PEPPOL infrastructure for exchanging VCDs and access related services. Requirement A method MUST be found how to allocate identifiers to the participants that use the infrastructure for transportation of VCDs and related services. Measurement Joined strategy and method with WP8 how to allocate identifiers among stakeholders and WP2 related services.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Description “Member States are building electronic identity management systems as a key element in their national strategy to deploy electronic services. Those national based systems are evolving without taking into account the requirement for interoperability that is needed in a cross-border scenario, resulting in a fragmented service that prevents the deployment of pan-European electronic services. In the public procurement business process, as in other electronic business processes, electronic identity is a key element for two main purposes: Avoiding unauthorized use of individuals identity Assuring to administrations that individuals are the persons they claim to be and have the mandate they claim to have” (Bausa O. and Boujraf A. 2009) Requirement Every party participating MUST be uniquely identifiable Description “Member States are building electronic identity management systems as a key element in their national strategy to deploy electronic services. Those national based systems are evolving without taking into account the requirement for interoperability that is needed in a cross-border scenario, resulting in a fragmented service that prevents the deployment of pan-European electronic services. In the public procurement business process, as in other electronic business processes, electronic identity is a key element for two main purposes: Avoiding unauthorized use of individuals identity Assuring to administrations that individuals are the persons they claim to be and have the mandate they claim to have” (Bausa O. and Boujraf A. 2009) Requirement Every party participating MUST be uniquely identifiable
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Description “To exchange electronic documents between parties in a cross-border environment, a major challenge is to find out the other party’s address and to understand what are his systems’ capabilities and requirements. Different levels of maturity and implementation of electronic business interfaces on public administrations and economic operators across Europe obliges to create mechanisms for discovering the supported standards, documents and endpoints for trader partners.” (Bausa O. and Boujraf A. 2009) Requirement In stage 4 it MUST be possible to discover the verified electronic address for an identified party. Description “To exchange electronic documents between parties in a cross-border environment, a major challenge is to find out the other party’s address and to understand what are his systems’ capabilities and requirements. Different levels of maturity and implementation of electronic business interfaces on public administrations and economic operators across Europe obliges to create mechanisms for discovering the supported standards, documents and endpoints for trader partners.” (Bausa O. and Boujraf A. 2009) Requirement In stage 4 it MUST be possible to discover the verified electronic address for an identified party.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The PEPPOL infrastructure will make it possible to exchange electronic business documents in a secure and reliable manner. It connects existing frameworks and networks, but also allows small businesses to participate in the electronic business processes if they have Internet access and can be securely identified. The PEPPOL infrastructure is being built on existing technologies and standards that have been tested and which have proven to be reliable and secure to use. For WP2 it will important to access the infrastructure from different perspectives. An economic operator for example has to submit a VCD via the PEPPOL infrastructure. A contracting authority may require access to a database hosted by a national service provider to receive an attestation by using a direct reference to it. Some web services may be connected to the infrastructure to add additional functionalities to a VCD or for example to receive mapping information from the pre-VCD mapping tool. Therefore different access points to the PEPPOL infrastructure may be necessary in particular: The PEPPOL infrastructure will make it possible to exchange electronic business documents in a secure and reliable manner. It connects existing frameworks and networks, but also allows small businesses to participate in the electronic business processes if they have Internet access and can be securely identified. The PEPPOL infrastructure is being built on existing technologies and standards that have been tested and which have proven to be reliable and secure to use. For WP2 it will important to access the infrastructure from different perspectives. An economic operator for example has to submit a VCD via the PEPPOL infrastructure. A contracting authority may require access to a database hosted by a national service provider to receive an attestation by using a direct reference to it. Some web services may be connected to the infrastructure to add additional functionalities to a VCD or for example to receive mapping information from the pre-VCD mapping tool. Therefore different access points to the PEPPOL infrastructure may be necessary in particular: Requirement It MUST be clarified which are the best access points for economic operators to use the infrastructure for transportation of a VCD package. Measurement Definition of access point within PEPPOL infrastructure and definition of VCD transportation system. Requirement It MUST be clarified whether the possibility exist to have access to web clients via the infrastructure in order to have access to specific web services which depend upon functionalities provided by the infrastructure. Measurement Discussion with WP8. National connection point Direct access points for SMEs Web services and client-side code generation in SOAP frameworks
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Description The VCD is just one part of bid that an economic operator may submit to a contracting authority. Therefore the systems in each Member State have to be analysed with regard to the prospects of separating both parts from each other. This may include for example to submit a VCD via the PEPPOL infrastructure while submitting other parts or the bid itself via secondary channels or whether tendering platforms enable such separation. Requirement It MUST be clarified whether tendering platforms enable a separated submission of a VCD and the bid itself. Measurement Result of national system analysis. Requirement The PEPPOL infrastructure MUST have the capability to submit a VCD to a contracting authority or tendering platform. Measurement Functionality for submitting VCD to a contracting authority or tendering platform. Requirement The PEPPOL infrastructure SHOULD have the capability to submit a bid which contains a VCD. Measurement Discussion with WP8 about the prospects to have a functionality for submitting a bid to a contracting authority or tendering platform that includes a VCD. ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Description “Ensuring that information is accessible only to those authorized to have access and in a predefined date is mandatory by the European Directive in some public procurement processes. Specifically, contracting authorities must provide a way to receive electronic tenders and ensure those electronic tenders are not accessed until the prescribed time for opening them. Annex X of the Directive 2004/18/EC lists the requirements relating to devices for the electronic receipt of tenders, requests for participation and plans and projects in contests.” (Bausa O. and Boujraf A. 2009) Requirement It MUST be possible to guarantee confidentiality for specific documents. Description “Ensuring that information is accessible only to those authorized to have access and in a predefined date is mandatory by the European Directive in some public procurement processes. Specifically, contracting authorities must provide a way to receive electronic tenders and ensure those electronic tenders are not accessed until the prescribed time for opening them. Annex X of the Directive 2004/18/EC lists the requirements relating to devices for the electronic receipt of tenders, requests for participation and plans and projects in contests.” (Bausa O. and Boujraf A. 2009) Requirement It MUST be possible to guarantee confidentiality for specific documents.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Description WP1 assumes that all actors can sign inside their corporate infrastructure. The e-tendering platform must offer download functionality for forms and other material to be filled in, signed, and returned by an economic operator. A validation service may be a technical service or a Validation Authority (VA) that takes on liability and acts as a trust anchor of its own. Validation services may be chained in order to serve requests that cannot be handled by the “local” validation service. The VCD essentially relies on such validation service in order to guarantee data integrity and confidentially of a VCD (cf. PEPPOL WP1-D1.1). Requirement It MUST be possible to apply the validation service provided by WP1 to a VCD in order to enable economic operators to guarantee data integrity and confidentiality of a VCD. Measurement Testing of validation service with VCD within the PEPPOL infrastructure. Requirement It MUST be clarified whether the VCD can use the validation service of WP1 if the transportation infrastructure is not used by an economic operator. Measurement Discussion with WP1. Description WP1 assumes that all actors can sign inside their corporate infrastructure. The e-tendering platform must offer download functionality for forms and other material to be filled in, signed, and returned by an economic operator. A validation service may be a technical service or a Validation Authority (VA) that takes on liability and acts as a trust anchor of its own. Validation services may be chained in order to serve requests that cannot be handled by the “local” validation service. The VCD essentially relies on such validation service in order to guarantee data integrity and confidentially of a VCD (cf. PEPPOL WP1-D1.1). Requirement It MUST be possible to apply the validation service provided by WP1 to a VCD in order to enable economic operators to guarantee data integrity and confidentiality of a VCD. Measurement Testing of validation service with VCD within the PEPPOL infrastructure. Requirement It MUST be clarified whether the VCD can use the validation service of WP1 if the transportation infrastructure is not used by an economic operator. Measurement Discussion with WP1.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Description “The purpose of an e-business transport infrastructure is to transport business documents between business partners. The transactions must be performed with high level of security and reliability. E.g. it must be ensured that business documents are actually delivered to the recipient party.” (Bausa O. and Boujraf A. 2009) Requirement Transport mechanisms between party’s addresses MUST be reliable. Description “The purpose of an e-business transport infrastructure is to transport business documents between business partners. The transactions must be performed with high level of security and reliability. E.g. it must be ensured that business documents are actually delivered to the recipient party.” (Bausa O. and Boujraf A. 2009) Requirement Transport mechanisms between party’s addresses MUST be reliable.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Description “The e-business transport infrastructure is to transport business documents between business partners. The transactions must be performed with high level of security. E.g. it must be ensured that third parties cannot intercept and read or modify the business documents.” (Bausa O. and Boujraf A. 2009). Requirement Transport mechanisms between party’s addresses MUST be secure. Description “The e-business transport infrastructure is to transport business documents between business partners. The transactions must be performed with high level of security. E.g. it must be ensured that third parties cannot intercept and read or modify the business documents.” (Bausa O. and Boujraf A. 2009). Requirement Transport mechanisms between party’s addresses MUST be secure.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Description “Data integrity ensures data has not been altered or modified during any operation such as transport or storage. Integrity can be compromised both by malicious intervention from a third party or by errors in the transport or storage mechanisms. Despite legal issues about the integrity of certain documents such as the electronic invoices, it should be a general rule to assure the integrity of all documents exchanged between partners.” (Bausa O. and Boujraf A. 2009). Requirement It MUST be assured that business document instances are consistent and correct during transportation, validation or transformation operations. The report documents various attestations of the Member States which are arranged according to the Articles 45 to 50 of Directive 2004/18/EC (Siemens_and_Time.lex 2007): The first interim report – national country profiles – of the preliminary study of DG Markt comprises a list of qualification documents typically used in tendering procedures in the Member States. The report documents various attestations of the Member States which are arranged according to the Articles 45 to 50 of Directive 2004/18/EC (Siemens_and_Time.lex 2007): The study may follow up with a “proposal for a tool for a mutual information mechanism on the electronic provision of certificates and attestations usually required in public procurement procedures” which shall be aligned with our approach of a pre-VCD mapping tool. The above mentioned evidences thereby will be further described by templates that define the structural data of evidence for example the: Evidence of the Economic Operator’s identity (e.g. Extracts of commercial records or equivalent documents) Evidence of absence of conviction for natural persons (e.g. Extracts of judicial records or equivalent documents) Evidence of absence of conviction for legal persons (e.g. Extracts of judicial records or equivalent documents) Evidence of non-bankruptcy and financial status (e.g. Declarations from commercial courts or equivalent documents) Evidence of compliance with fiscal obligations (e.g. Declarations from fiscal services or equivalent documents) Evidence of compliance with social security obligations (e.g. Declarations from social security services or equivalent documents) Evidence of economic and financial standing (e.g. Bank statements, turnover sheets or equivalent documents) Evidence of professional qualification (e.g. Extracts from professional registers or equivalent documents) Evidence of technical and/or professional ability (e.g. Diplomas, descriptions of goods and services or equivalent documents) Evidence of adequate insurance (e.g. Insurance certificates or equivalent documents) Evidence of quality assurance (e.g. certification by auditing bodies of goods and services or equivalent documents) Evidence of environmental compliance (e.g. certification by auditing bodies of environmental practices or equivalent documents) Type of document Name of document Issuing entity Source reference Validity period Time of issuance Mandatory or optional Frequency of use Flexibility of submission Semantic characteristics Semantic characteristics Validation requirements

ground_truth_requirements.text: The major objective of this deliverable is to develop a concept for a VCD and to define major requirements that shape this VCD concept. Therefore we have defined different scenarios and a vision that contains several different levels of maturity. With the staged maturity model we intend to reach a wide flexibility and openness of such a solution encompassing all Member States to implement their individual preferences. Nevertheless the concept provides common grounds such as the pre-VCD mapping and structural data of evidences which ensures interoperability of evidences and their perception across borders. Beside we defined a set of requirements on organisational, semantic, technical and legal level that clarify general aspect of VCD development and deployment. To support the mutual recognition of evidences across border the VCD requires an underlying semantic model that enables a standardized classification and mapping between them (pre-VCD mapping tool). If the contracting authority specifies the relevant evidences which are related to criteria, the data can be processed automatically in order to look-up the evidences required within another Member State. The standard structure of the VCD, with the semantic enrichment of its structure then provides a European-wide understanding and format of company formal evidence. The different levels of maturity can be applied in dependence on the willingness and readiness of every Member State. The aim is to support basic interoperability and exchange of documents on the lower maturity levels and leading towards direct electronic exchange of evidence in the higher maturity levels. Thereby each maturity level is built upon the lower ones thus allowing integration and stepwise advancement. The VCD shall ensure that a significant part of the public procurement processes can be made through electronic cross-border transactions. Therefore the national focus of a VCD needs to be enhanced through a cross border perspective. The VCD will provide the possibility to utilize evidences used in one Member State and to submit them across borders to any European contracting authority. A VCD provides a single point of contact for an economic operator that enables him to invoke and manage different services to create a VCD. A service (re-)compiles the VCD package for the economic operator or even a group of economic operators. The VCD services merge data from several sources into a single container and make this data available and understandable to the actors involved. Economic operators will be enabled to perform checks with regard to the existence and validity of the requested evidences in the VCD package. Structural data of evidence will provide guidance for foreign contracting authorities to understand the evidences contained within a VCD and they can trust that general information since it is provided by national VCD service providers that take the role of trusted parties. Context specific data will be provided on the higher levels to increase decision support. ground_truth_requirements.text: There are also some weaknesses that can be identified for the overall concept. These weaknesses are mostly derived from the complexity of the domain and how the VCD solution reacts on this domain. One of the biggest weaknesses is probably the development of context specific data for the evidences within a VCD. The development of context specific data shall ensure the automatic processing of electronic attestations without regard to linguistic barriers. The development of standards and context specific vocabularies is time consuming and requires long term involvement of user groups that will make use of these standards. The VCD contains several different kinds of evidences and to develop common standards for each one cannot be a major task and focus of this work package and should be done by initiative such as CEN BII, BRITE, XBRL or ECRIS. Since CEN BII workshop is close to an end it will probably not work out to do this development within its scope. Nevertheless we will start to develop comm of contents and marginal variances in their contents. Furthermore we rely on structural data of evidences that describe and characterize evidence from a more generic point of view. The possibility to replace the full document translation with a “selective translation” through context specific data (keywords and major contents) bares certain risks. The “selective translation” could be arranged in multiple languages in particular when context specific data belongs to a common agreed dictionary of expressions. Nevertheless the acceptance of those selective translations instead of certified translations may be questionable for contracting authorities. Thus the resolution of language barriers and linguistic issues cannot be completely managed by a VCD in short term and may require adding official translations beside selective translations. Provision of context specific data is a precondition to establish direct information exchange between attestation providers and contracting authorities. This direct information exchange would provide many opportunities with regard to increase the actuality of evidence, to lower the transaction costs of the parties involved and to reduce the time to create a VCD. Nevertheless such direct exchange is very difficult to establish because a lot of entities and attestation services have to be federated, access policies have to be developed, legal uncertainties have to be resolved and political guidelines have to be considered. To circumvent these problems the VCD provides in particular in stage 4 a guideline on how to move forward in order to reach this ambitious aim but it will not be a major focus of a VCD as such. The VCD will be dependent on a number of services that rely on manual and semi-automated input. Thus the VCD system will be primary a tool for the economic operator to arrange a VCD package instance which is conform according to the requirements stated in particular call for tender which he can fill through additional inputs. A direct access to attestation via the national VCD service provider can only be provided for those public attestations that are already available in electronic format. The VCD will not enforce any development of electronic attestation on a national level as this is a clear national duty and is depended upon the development of context specific data. Nevertheless existing attestations may be extended through additional annotations that describe these documents generically (structural data of evidences). Unclear yet is the role of Member States that have already implemented Article 5224. The scope of mutual recognition needs to be further clarified with regard to acceptance of these procedures across borders and the liability of those trusted third parties across borders. It needs to be further defined how a VCD can support countries that have implemented Article 52 but since there are currently no pilots in our work package that have such procedures already implemented we have left this out of scope. Neverton representations (context specific data) for evidences that have an, easy structure, low quantityheless we see a great opportunity to combine both ideas as shown in stage 4. One of the biggest weaknesses is probably the development of context specific data for the evidences within a VCD. brd_document:
# Business Requirements Document (BRD)

**Project ID:** 2010-blitdraft
**Document Status:** Draft
**Prepared By:** Business Analysis Team
**Intended Audience:** Project Stakeholders, Development Team, QA/QC Team, Technical Lead

---

## 1. PROBLEM STATEMENT

The organization operates an internal enterprise web application (referred to herein as "the System") used by staff across multiple divisions, lab locations, and organizational designator codes. The System supports role-based workflows that are sensitive to HIPAA compliance obligations, indicating the organization operates in or adjacent to a healthcare or clinical laboratory environment.

Currently, the administration of user accounts within the System is a manual, error-prone process that lacks sufficient safeguards. Specifically, the following pain points have been identified:

- **User provisioning is inconsistent.** There is no standardized mechanism to create new user accounts with pre-defined role configurations, forcing administrators to manually assign roles, divisions, designator codes, and lab locations for every new user — increasing the risk of misconfiguration and unauthorized access.
- **No Active Directory integration for user validation.** The System does not verify whether a prospective new user is active within the company's Active Directory before account creation, resulting in accounts being created for terminated or inactive employees.
- **Duplicate user accounts are not prevented.** The System does not check for the existence of a user before creating a new record, leading to duplicate entries that corrupt reporting and access control.
- **Navigation and discoverability are poor.** Users report difficulty locating system functions, and there is no consistent, structured help system to guide users through operational tasks.
- **Logging and error notification are inadequate.** System errors are not reliably captured in a centralized log, and the Client Services team is not automatically notified when users encounter system error pages, delaying incident response.
- **Development and release practices are inconsistent.** There are no enforced coding standards, no formal build labeling process, and no structured release note distribution, making it difficult to trace defects and manage change.

These gaps collectively increase operational risk, administrative overhead, and the likelihood of compliance violations under HIPAA. The organization requires a structured enhancement to the System that addresses user management, navigation, online help, error handling, and development governance.

---

## 2. BUSINESS OBJECTIVES & SUCCESS CRITERIA

The following objectives define what the System must achieve upon delivery of this project. Success criteria are stated in measurable terms wherever the requirements permit.

### Objective 1: Streamline and Safeguard User Provisioning
The System must enable administrators to create new user accounts accurately, efficiently, and safely, with built-in validation against both the internal user database and Active Directory.

**Success Criteria:**
- 100% of new user creation attempts are validated against Active Directory before the account is saved.
- 100% of duplicate user creation attempts are blocked with a system-generated error message.
- Administrators can create a new user from a template in fewer steps than full manual entry, with role inheritance confirmed at save time.
- Zero user accounts are saved without at least one role, one division, one designator code, and one lab location associated.

### Objective 2: Improve System Navigation and Usability
Users must be able to locate and access system functions quickly through a consistent, hyperlink-driven navigation model aligned with the User Interface Specification (UIS).

**Success Criteria:**
- All functional pages are reachable via clearly labeled hyperlinks from the navigation layer.
- Required fields on all data entry forms are visually marked (red asterisk) with no exceptions.
- User acceptance testing (UAT) confirms navigation usability with no critical navigation defects outstanding at release.

### Objective 3: Deliver a Comprehensive Online Help System
Users must have access to context-sensitive, searchable online help that reduces reliance on informal support channels.

**Success Criteria:**
- A help pop-up window is accessible from every page in the System.
- The help system includes full-text search, an index, and a glossary of industry-related terms.
- The default help landing page loads the system overview topic without additional user action.
- Help pages conform to the organization's approved color scheme and display the company logo consistently.

### Objective 4: Establish Reliable Error Logging and Notification
All system errors must be captured in a centralized log file, and the Client Services team must be automatically notified of user-facing error events.

**Success Criteria:**
- 100% of system errors, warnings, and informational events are written to the external log file on the application server.
- An automated email notification is sent to the Client Services distribution list within minutes of a user being directed to a system error page.
- No error event results in a silent failure (i.e., unlogged and unnotified).

### Objective 5: Enforce Consistent Development and Release Governance
The development lifecycle must follow defined standards for coding, build management, source control, testing, and deployment.

**Success Criteria:**
- Every build deployed to the Staging environment is labeled in source control and accompanied by release notes distributed to the full Project Team.
- No build is deployed to the production environment without Technical Owner/Lead signoff.
- All production deployments occur within the approved maintenance window (Tuesdays, 7:00 PM – 7:00 AM).
- QA/QC regression testing is completed against every scheduled build prior to production release.

---

## 3. STAKEHOLDERS

| Stakeholder | Role | Primary Need |
|---|---|---|
| **System Administrator** | Manages user accounts, roles, and access control within the System | Ability to create, configure, and validate user accounts efficiently and accurately, with template-based provisioning |
| **End User (General Staff)** | Performs day-to-day operational tasks within the System | Intuitive navigation, clear help resources, and reliable system behavior |
| **Project Stakeholders (Business Owners)** | Approve scope, UI designs, and release readiness | Early visibility into UI mockups and the ability to provide reasonable input on interface design before development is finalized |
| **Technical Owner / Technical Lead** | Oversees architecture, code quality, and release authorization | Enforcement of coding standards, code review compliance, and final signoff authority before any production deployment |
| **Development Team** | Designs, builds, and maintains the System | Clear requirements, defined coding standards, and a structured build and release process |
| **QA/QC Team** | Validates system functionality and regression integrity | Sufficient time and access to perform regression testing and UAT against each build |
| **Client Services Team** | Responds to user-reported system issues | Automated email notification when users encounter system error pages, enabling rapid incident response |
| **IT / Active Directory Administrator** | Maintains the corporate Active Directory | The System must integrate with Active Directory to validate user status; no changes to AD are expected from this project |
| **Compliance / HIPAA Officer** | Ensures the System meets regulatory obligations | Confirmation that all new and modified functionality adheres to existing HIPAA compliance capabilities |

---

## 4. CURRENT STATE vs. FUTURE STATE

### Current State

The System currently operates as a functional but administratively immature enterprise application. The following limitations characterize the current state:

- **User Management:** Administrators create user accounts entirely by hand, with no template mechanism, no duplicate detection, and no Active Directory validation. Roles, divisions, designator codes, and lab locations must be manually assigned each time, with no enforcement that all required associations are present before saving.
- **Navigation:** System functions are accessible but not consistently organized or presented through a standardized hyperlink-and-description model aligned to a formal UIS. Users must rely on institutional knowledge to locate features.
- **Help System:** No structured online help system exists. Users seeking guidance must consult colleagues, email support, or reference external documentation, increasing support burden on Client Services.
- **Error Handling:** System errors may or may not be captured in a log, and Client Services has no automated mechanism to learn when a user has been directed to an error page. Incident detection is reactive and dependent on user-initiated reports.
- **Development Governance:** Coding standards are not formally defined or consistently applied. Build labeling, release notes, and structured code review processes are informal or absent. Production deployments do not follow a defined maintenance window.
- **Compliance:** The System maintains existing HIPAA compliance capabilities, but new functionality introduced without a formal compliance review process risks introducing gaps.

### Future State

Upon completion of this project, the System will:

- **User Management:** Provide administrators with a guided, validated user creation workflow that checks Active Directory status and detects duplicates before saving. Administrators may create users manually or from a pre-configured template that automatically inherits role settings. All required associations (role, division, designator code, lab location) are enforced at save time, with clear error messaging for any missing data.
- **Navigation:** Present all system functions through a consistent, UIS-aligned hyperlink navigation model. Required fields are universally marked with red asterisks. Confirmation, warning, and error messages follow a standard pattern across all interactions.
- **Help System:** Deliver a context-sensitive, pop-up help window accessible from every page, featuring a searchable topic library, an index, a glossary, and a default landing page showing the system overview. Help pages conform to the organization's visual standards.
- **Error Handling:** Log all errors, warnings, and informational messages to a centralized external log file on the application server. Automatically notify the Client Services distribution list via email whenever a user is directed to a system error page.
- **Development Governance:** Operate under formally defined coding standards, a structured build and labeling process, mandatory code reviews by the Technical Lead, weekly Staging deployments with release notes, and production deployments restricted to the approved maintenance window with Technical Owner/Lead signoff.
- **Compliance:** All new functionality introduced by this project will be developed in conformance with the organization's existing HIPAA compliance posture.

### Gap Summary

| Dimension | Current State Gap | Future State Resolution |
|---|---|---|
| User Provisioning | Manual, unvalidated, no duplicate detection | Validated, template-driven, enforced associations |
| Active Directory Integration | None | Real-time status check at user creation |
| Navigation | Inconsistent, undocumented | UIS-aligned hyperlink model |
| Online Help | Absent | Full help system with search, index, glossary |
| Error Logging | Incomplete/informal | Centralized external log file |
| Error Notification | Manual/reactive | Automated email to Client Services |
| Development Governance | Informal | Defined standards, code review, build labeling, release notes |
| HIPAA Compliance | Maintained for existing features only | Extended to all new functionality |

---

## 5. BUSINESS RULES

The following domain-specific rules govern how the System must behave. These rules are non-negotiable and must be reflected in design, development, and testing.

### User Management Rules

**BR-UM-01:** A user account may only be created by a user who holds administrator privileges within the System. General users do not have access to user creation functions.

**BR-UM-02:** Before a new user account is saved, the System must verify that the prospective user does not already exist in the System by searching both the display name and username columns of the user table. If a match is found, the account creation must be blocked and an error message displayed.

**BR-UM-03:** Before a new user account is saved, the System must verify that the prospective user is currently active in the corporate Active Directory. If the user is inactive or not found in Active Directory, the account creation must be blocked and an error message displayed.

**BR-UM-04:** A new user account cannot be saved unless the administrator has associated at least one role, one division, one designator code, and one lab location with the account. Attempting to save without all four associations must trigger a standard error/warning message.

**BR-UM-05:** A new user account cannot be saved unless the User Name and Display Name fields in the User Information section are populated. These are mandatory fields and must be visually marked with a red asterisk.

**BR-UM-06:** When a new user is created from a template, the new user record automatically inherits all role settings defined in that template. The administrator may add additional roles manually during the creation process, but may not remove template-inherited roles during initial creation.

**BR-UM-07:** If an administrator clicks Cancel during the user creation process, all data entered during that session must be cleared. No partial user record may be persisted.

**BR-UM-08:** If an administrator clicks Close without having entered any data, the System must redirect the user to the designated default page (the "xxx Page") without displaying a data-loss warning.

**BR-UM-09:** User account data, including all associated roles and division assignments, must be persisted to the SQL Server 2008 database upon successful save.

### Navigation and UI Rules

**BR-UI-01:** All system functions must be accessible via hyperlinks accompanied by descriptive labels, as defined in the relevant User Interface Specification (UIS).

**BR-UI-02:** The default tab displayed upon entering the user creation workflow is the designated "xxx Tab." Users may navigate to any other tab at any point during the creation process.

**BR-UI-03:** All required fields across all data entry forms must be marked with a red asterisk. No required field may be left unmarked.

**BR-UI-04:** The System must generate a standard confirmation message upon successful data save. A warning or caution message must be generated when the user clicks Cancel or Close during an active data entry session.

### Help System Rules

**BR-HLP-01:** The help system must open in a pop-up window and must not replace or obscure the operational page the user is working on.

**BR-HLP-02:** The default help topic displayed upon opening the help window is the "[System Name] Overview" page located in the Getting Started section.

**BR-HLP-03:** Help topic navigation must be available via hyperlinks in the left frame of the help pop-up window.

**BR-HLP-04:** All help pages must display the company logo in the top-right corner and must use the organization's approved color scheme and background.

**BR-HLP-05:** The help system must include full-text search, a topic index, and a glossary of industry-related and company-specific terms.

### Error Handling and Logging Rules

**BR-ERR-01:** All errors, warnings, and informational events must be written to an external log file residing on the application server. Silent failures are not permitted.

**BR-ERR-02:** When a user is directed to the System's error page, an error entry must be written to the external log file and an automated notification email must be sent to the Client Services email distribution list.

**BR-ERR-03:** Existing source code that is not otherwise being modified for this project is not required to be retrofitted to use the external log file. However, any source code modified to implement a requirement under this project must be updated to use the external log file.

### Development and Release Governance Rules

**BR-DEV-01:** All new development must use the designated programming language on the .NET 3.5 platform. Established open-source frameworks are preferred over proprietary custom components.

**BR-DEV-02:** The Technical Lead must conduct a code review of every system change before that change may be committed to source control.

**BR-DEV-03:** Every scheduled build must be labeled in the source control system. Builds deployed to any environment must be checked out using the defined label.

**BR-DEV-04:** The Development Team must perform weekly integrations and deploy updates to the Staging environment on a weekly cadence.

**BR-DEV-05:** Release notes must be prepared and distributed via email to the full Project Team following every build deployed to the Staging environment.

**BR-DEV-06:** No build may be deployed to the production environment without written signoff from the Technical Owner/Lead.

**BR-DEV-07:** All production deployments must be scheduled within the approved maintenance window: Tuesdays between 7:00 PM and 7:00 AM.

**BR-DEV-08:** The QA/QC Team must perform regression testing against every scheduled build. No build may proceed to production without completed regression testing.

**BR-DEV-09:** User Acceptance Testing (UAT) must be completed against built functionalities before any deployment to the production environment. Sufficient time must be allocated for UAT in the project schedule.

**BR-DEV-10:** Existing source code that is not being modified for this project is not required to be refactored to meet current coding standards. However, any source code modified under this project must be brought into conformance with the defined coding standards.

### HIPAA Compliance Rules

**BR-HIPAA-01:** All existing HIPAA compliance capabilities of the System must be preserved. No change introduced by this project may degrade or remove existing compliance controls.

**BR-HIPAA-02:** All new functionality introduced by this project must be designed and implemented in conformance with the organization's HIPAA compliance requirements.

---

## 6. SCOPE

### In Scope

The following capabilities and activities are within scope for Project 2010-blitdraft:

1. **User Management Module Enhancements**
   - Administrator-only user creation workflow with manual and template-based creation paths
   - Active Directory integration for user status validation at account creation time
   - Duplicate user detection via search of the System's user table
   - Enforcement of required associations (role, division, designator code, lab location) and required fields (User Name, Display Name) before save
   - Role inheritance from user templates with the ability to add additional roles manually
   - Standard confirmation, warning, and error messaging throughout the user creation workflow
   - Cancel and Close behavior with appropriate data clearing and page redirection

2. **Navigation Framework**
   - Implementation of a UIS-aligned hyperlink-and-description navigation model across the System
   - Consistent application of red asterisk indicators on all required fields
   - Standard message patterns (confirmation, warning, error) applied system-wide

3. **Online Help System**
   - Context-sensitive pop-up help window accessible from all pages
   - Default landing page (System Overview under Getting Started)
   - Left-frame hyperlink navigation within the help window
   - Full-text search, topic index, and glossary
   - Company logo and approved visual styling on all help pages

4. **Error Logging and Notification**
   - External log file on the application server capturing all errors, warnings, and informational messages
   - Automated email notification to Client Services distribution list upon user-facing error page events
   - Retrofitting of modified source code to use the external log file

5. **Development and Release Governance**
   - Definition and documentation of coding standards
   - Establishment of build labeling, code review, weekly Staging deployment, and release note distribution processes
   - Enforcement of the production deployment maintenance window and Technical Owner/Lead signoff requirement
   - QA/QC regression testing and UAT processes formalized within the project lifecycle

6. **HIPAA Compliance**
   - Compliance review of all new functionality introduced by this project
   - Preservation of all existing HIPAA compliance controls

7. **Stakeholder UI Review Process**
   - Demonstration of UI mockups to project stakeholders early in development
   - Accommodation of reasonable UI adjustments within schedule constraints

### Out of Scope

The following items are explicitly excluded from this project:

- Changes to the corporate Active Directory structure, schema, or administration processes
- Migration from SQL Server 2008 to any other database platform
- Refactoring of existing source code that is not otherwise being modified under this project (for either coding standards or log file adoption)
- Development of new system modules or functional areas beyond those described above
- Changes to HIPAA compliance policies or procedures themselves (the System must conform to existing policies)
- Mobile or native application development; the System remains a web-based application
- Integration with any external system other than the corporate Active Directory

---

## 7. CONSTRAINTS

### Technical Constraints

**TC-01: Platform.** All new development must target the .NET 3.5 platform. No upgrade to a newer .NET version is authorized under this project.

**TC-02: Database.** The System must continue to use a single SQL Server 2008 database instance. No database platform migration or multi-database architecture is permitted.

**TC-03: Programming Language.** Development must use the designated language as established by the Technical Lead and consistent with the existing codebase.

**TC-04: Open Source Preference.** Where third-party components are needed, established open-source frameworks must be used in preference to proprietary or custom-built components.

**TC-05: Source Control.** All code changes must be managed through the organization's existing source control system. Every scheduled build must be labeled in source control, and builds must be produced from labeled checkouts only.

**TC-06: Existing Code Modification Limits.** Existing source code that is not being modified to implement a requirement under this project is not subject to coding standard refactoring or log file adoption requirements. This constraint is intended to limit scope creep and regression risk.

### Regulatory Constraints

**RC-01: HIPAA Compliance.** The System operates in a healthcare-adjacent environment and is subject to HIPAA regulations. All existing compliance controls must be preserved, and all new functionality must be developed in conformance with HIPAA requirements. Any design decision that could affect protected health information (PHI) handling must be reviewed against HIPAA obligations before implementation.

### Operational Constraints

**OC-01: Production Deployment Window.** Production deployments are restricted to Tuesdays between 7:00 PM and 7:00 AM. No exceptions are permitted without explicit approval from the Technical Owner/Lead and relevant business stakeholders.

**OC-02: Technical Owner/Lead Signoff.** No build may be released to the production environment without documented signoff from the Technical Owner/Lead. This is a hard gate in the release process.

**OC-03: UAT Time Allocation.** The project schedule must include sufficient time for User Acceptance Testing prior to each production release. UAT cannot be compressed to the point where meaningful testing is not possible.

**OC-04: Stakeholder UI Review.** UI mockups for new functionality must be demonstrated to project stakeholders early in the development cycle. Reasonable UI adjustments requested by stakeholders must be accommodated within the project schedule.

**OC-05: Code Review Gate.** No code change may be committed to source control without a completed code review by the Technical Lead. This constraint applies to all changes regardless of size or perceived risk.

---

## 8. ASSUMPTIONS & DEPENDENCIES

### Assumptions

**A-01:** The corporate Active Directory is operational, accessible from the application server, and contains accurate, up-to-date records of all current and former employees, including their active/inactive status.

**A-02:** The organization has an existing, defined HIPAA compliance framework that the System currently adheres to. This project does not require the creation of new compliance policies, only conformance with existing ones.

**A-03:** A User Interface Specification (UIS) document exists or will be produced in parallel with this project and will serve as the authoritative reference for navigation structure, required fields, tab ordering, and page layouts. The BRD defers to the UIS for specific field names, tab names, and page identifiers (referenced as "xxx" in the requirements).

**A-04:** The organization has an existing source control system in place that supports build labeling and code checkout by label. No procurement or setup of a new source control system is required.

**A-05:** A Client Services email distribution list exists and is maintained by the organization. The System will send automated notifications to this list without requiring the project to create or manage the list.

**A-06:** The application server infrastructure is in place and capable of hosting an external log file with sufficient storage and access controls. No new server infrastructure procurement is required under this project.

**A-07:** The organization has an existing SQL Server 2008 database instance that the System currently uses. No database provisioning is required.

**A-08:** Project stakeholders are available and willing to participate in UI mockup reviews early in the development cycle and will provide timely feedback to avoid schedule delays.

**A-09:** The QA/QC Team has sufficient capacity and access to the Staging environment to perform regression testing against each weekly build within the project timeline.

**A-10:** User templates referenced in the user creation workflow already exist or will be created by administrators prior to the template-based creation feature being used in production. The project is responsible for building the template inheritance mechanism, not for populating templates.

**A-11:** The designated "xxx Tab" (default tab in the user creation workflow) and the "xxx Page" (redirect destination upon close with no data) will be formally named and specified in the UIS before development of the user management module begins.

### Dependencies

**D-01: UIS Completion.** The User Interface Specification must be finalized and approved before detailed design and development of the navigation framework and user management module can begin. Delays in UIS delivery will directly impact the development schedule.

**D-02: Active Directory Access.** The Development Team requires network-level access to the corporate Active Directory from the development and staging environments to build and test the user validation integration. This access must be provisioned by IT before development of the user creation validation feature begins.

**D-03: Coding Standards Definition.** The Technical Lead must define and publish the coding standards document before development begins. All development work is dependent on this document being available.

**D-04: Help Content Authorship.** The online help system requires authored content (topic pages, glossary entries, index entries). A subject matter expert or technical writer must be identified and engaged to produce this content in parallel with the help system's technical development. Delays in content delivery will delay the help system release.

**D-05: Client Services Distribution List.** The Client Services email distribution list address must be provided to the Development Team before the error notification feature can be configured and tested.

**D-06: Technical Owner/Lead Availability.** The Technical Owner/Lead must be available to conduct code reviews and provide build signoff on a schedule consistent with the weekly build cadence. Unavailability of the Technical Owner/Lead is a schedule risk that must be managed proactively.

**D-07: Stakeholder Availability for UAT.** Business stakeholders and end users must be available to participate in UAT within the time windows allocated in the project schedule. Late or incomplete UAT participation may delay production releases.

Any proposed changes to this baseline must be submitted through the project's formal change control process and approved by the relevant stakeholders before implementation.*
